commentText: Northwolf Well put. I've got a straight aim for the cold calls alongside linkedin outreach. As my niche and ICP is very well sniped in, and i had an epic lead book from that first week testing ads at 11bucks daily... The past 10 days has generated none, but playing with video now. Figured continuing
reply:
That’s the move, brother.

Cold is the spear. Content and ads are the fire around it.

If your ICP is that dialed in, stay ruthless with outreach and let the ads teach you what message bites hardest. One booked call off 11 bucks tells you there’s something there.

Now it’s just about sharpening the angle, bro. Keep testing. Keep swinging. You’re closer than it looks.

commentText: Leijonmarck heavily feel you on the socializing part, on that topic, how do you end up finding a balance?
reply: Honestly I’ve had to make it intentional, Lea. If I just “hope” it happens, work eats the whole week. So for me it’s been about protecting a little space for real human time before the grind takes over. Crazy how much the nervous system needs that too.

commentText: If your offer is a one liner like "I take self-improvement coaches from $0 to $10K per month in 42 days with my 'Reverse Engineered $10K Method' or they don't pay" then you do tell it even to your neighboor because you lose nothing and create massive curiousity by putting it out there every time you
reply:
Facts, bro.

When the offer is that clean, you say it with your chest every chance you get.

Clarity pulls. Mystery keeps them leaning in. Scott nailed that.

commentText: Interesting take: DIscipline the inner battle between your new identity and your old one.Anyone agree?
reply:
Facts, bro.

Discipline is the bridge between who you were and who you’re becoming.

That war is real. Win enough small rounds and the new man takes over.
